mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Nuts Peanuts Contain Zinc, Please: Null hypothesis, Parameter, Conditions, Z-score, P-value, State conclusion.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: The test assumes the sample is large enough (n > 30) for the Central Limit Theorem to apply, making the sampling distribution approximately normal.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The core logic is to compute the probability (P-value) of observing your sample data if the null hypothesis is true. A small P-value provides evidence against the null.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The Z-test statistic measures how many standard errors the sample statistic is from the hypothesized population parameter stated in the null.
